steps.text: Evaluate Selectivity
Evaluate Instrument calibration
Evaluate Bias/Trueness
Evaluate Precision
Evaluate Quantitation limits and range
Evaluate Detection limits
Evaluate selectivity by testing appropriate matrix blanks and fortified (spiked) matrix blanks.
Test a worst-case sample matrix, as defined in the scope of a method, during both intra- and interlaboratory validation studies.
Study actual samples of material containing an undetermined amount of native analyte to evaluate method selectivity.
Use a combination of procedures or techniques that are based on different measurement principles to substantiate selectivity.
Thoroughly describe the basis for quantitative selectivity measures if they are used.

preconditions.text: Adequate representations of matrix blanks cannot be obtained or prepared

constraints.text: Matrix blanks should contain the chemical species, other than the analyte(s) of interest, which are reasonably expected to be present in an actual sample.

steps.text: Thoroughly test and justify the calibration approach during an intralaboratory validation study.
Test the calibration approach during an interlaboratory validation study.
Document the instrument calibration procedure in a method validation report.
Estimate method bias by measuring reference materials, comparing results to an alternate method, or analyzing spiked materials.

preconditions.text: Calibration performance characteristics are deemed acceptable during the intralaboratory study.

steps.text: Verify defined limits (such as those based on specifications for precision) by testing materials containing analyte at the claimed LOQ.
Define the term 'detection limit'.
Provide a description of how the detection limit was evaluated during method validation.
Verify limits derived from mathematical definitions or statistical models by testing materials containing analyte at the claimed detection level.

preconditions.text: If limits are derived from mathematical definitions or statistical models

steps.text: Complete ruggedness testing.
Identify critical control points in the documented method.
Conduct and complete an interlaboratory method validation study.
Document any deviations from the interlaboratory study protocol that occur.
Publish the EPA method for general use.

preconditions.text: A successful intralaboratory study must be completed.
The issuing entity must demonstrate a pressing programmatic need for publishing a national method.
An interlaboratory method validation study must be completed.

constraints.text: The fractional factorial experimental design referenced herein is not intended for method optimization purposes.
Deviations from the interlaboratory study protocol should be strongly discouraged.

steps.text: Prepare a method validation report.
Address background information on method development in the report.
Address a description of the actual method validation techniques in the report.
Address any changes made to the method as a result of the validation studies in the report.
Address any recommendations for future work in the report.
Address the purpose of the method, including measurement objectives and intended use of the data.

Address the scope and applicability, defining the range of method performance studies, components validated, analytes/matrices, range of analyte levels, limitations, assumptions, and data quality objectives.
Address selectivity, demonstrating the ability of the method to yield useful data for analytes, levels, and matrices, especially at action levels of concern.
Address instrument calibration in the report.

constraints.text: The method validation report should be suitable for placing in the public docket.

steps.text: Generate sufficient data to demonstrate and justify the method-recommended calibration approach during method validation.
Evaluate trueness using samples that span the degree of analytical difficulty encompassed by the specified scope and applicability.
Evaluate methods designed for demonstrating compliance with regulatory requirements for both repeatability (within-lab) and reproducibility (among labs).
Characterize analytical measurement uncertainty over a quantitation range.
Peer review the EPA method of analysis in accordance with the EPA Science Technology Policy Council Peer Review Handbook or review it by a documented alternate procedure.

preconditions.text: Prior to publication of the EPA method of analysis

constraints.text: Ensure the robustness and technical viability of the method while considering stakeholder and expert input (if using an alternate review procedure).

steps.text: Specify the changed conditions when providing a statement of reproducibility.
When giving a measurement result, make clear whether it refers to the indication, the uncorrected result, or the corrected result, and whether several values are averaged.
